title: Digis recap at Otakon and Gendos hand
display_title: Digis recap at Otakon and Gendos hand
wikitext:
[]: Digi's recap at Otakon... (and Gendo's hand)             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue Aug 23, 2005 4:36 pm Otakon. It has become a word that I will associate with fond memories from now on. I'm not a stranger to anime conventions but Otakon has quickly become my favorite con. I made new friends while attending panels and lunch GTGs. I finally got a chance to put a face to the name of forum members as well as share in fun conversations. I was able to kick back and enjoy watching AMVs on the big screen. I was also able to empty my wallet as I bought a bunch of impractical merchandise in the dealer room. There's something to be said about 25,000 people attending an anime convention. It's something I considered an underground hobby for many years. I really liked the fact that I could let the anime geek in me let loose for a weekend. It's good to know there are others like me. The crowd...from 9am to midnight. Video 1. This is one of the video rooms where anime screenings and AMV overflows were played. These "smaller" rooms are larger than main showings at other Cons. The ballroom. The grand daddy. This is where I got a chance to enjoy AMV screenings, especially the Eva AMV Nightmare which won the Drama/Serious AMV category. It was a great setup. The pic does not do the room justice. It was HUGE. I used to despise cosplaying. mainly because I've seen some terrible work in the past. The last few cons I've been to have changed my attitude towards them. I've seen some excellent designs (paired with the correct persons body type and gender) at Otakon. Just because I know EvaMonkey LOVES Naruto... The Eva lunch on Friday. It was nice to meet up with Dan, Ron, Drew, Rachel, and Aaron (and entourage) for this little GTG. Ron really had the goods at this one... The mousepad. It was made of nice material but was a sorry excuse for one otherwise. I couldn't help myself. I always get caught up on buying impractical things when attending cons. My normal senses leave me when I enter through the doors. Reichu, these are for you. I know how you LOVE seeing Asuka and Rei out of character. Reichu, didn't you model a few Evas after these Xmas edition figures? Seeing how I am both an Evafreak and Narutard, I couldn't pass these up... The 10th Anniversary Eva panel. Although things did not turn out as expected all was not lost. It was great to meet quite a few forum members during the Friday lunch and before the panel. I was greeted promptly by Gendo... (and NO...I DID NOT CrossPlay as Ritsuko to merit this kind of response). This particular Gendo had a small radio that played the fantastic "It's Gendo" theme from Redeath along with other series tracks. The panel itself... From L to R: The charismatic but tardy-Otaprince, the gifted Eva librarian-Reichu, and the illustrious, strongly opinionated, giver of seal of approvals-EvaMonkey. Yeah, you can say it was packed... My favorite part. I liked the Cosplayers. The 1% Eva cosplay fanboy in me was overjoyed. The alternate EoE ending... That's pretty much my experience at Otakon in a nutshell. I look forward to meeting up with old and new forum members again next year.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:19 pm How much was the Eva-01 Figure? Is the the one with the Sachiel stand?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:24 pm sadsadshinji wrote: How much was the Eva-01 Figure? Is the the one with the Sachiel stand?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:29 pm Damn good prices, from what I have seen. And was the gun carried by the Gendo cosplayer a real airsoft?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:33 pm sadsadshinji wrote: Damn good prices, from what I have seen. And was the gun carried by the Gendo cosplayer a real airsoft?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:47 pm I am so fucking going next year. I'm writing that in blood on my ass as we speak.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:52 pm Ahhh, Otakon is so much bigger than AnimeNext. Sooo much bigger. I wanna go next year, but I'm already going to AnimeNext. Again. For the third time. But I'm cosplaying as Asuka. Maybe, if I'm not completely broke from AnimeNext, I'll be able to afford Otakon, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Tue Aug 23, 2005 6:03 pm sachiel wrote: Ahhh, Otakon is so much bigger than AnimeNext. Sooo much bigger. I wanna go next year, but I'm already going to AnimeNext. Again. For the third time. But I'm cosplaying as Asuka. Maybe, if I'm not completely broke from AnimeNext, I'll be able to afford Otakon, too.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Aug 23, 2005 6:11 pm Digitalex wrote: sachiel wrote: Ahhh, Otakon is so much bigger than AnimeNext. Sooo much bigger. I wanna go next year, but I'm already going to AnimeNext. Again. For the third time. But I'm cosplaying as Asuka. Maybe, if I'm not completely broke from AnimeNext, I'll be able to afford Otakon, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 23, 2005 7:18 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: I am so fucking going next year. I'm writing that in blood on my ass as we speak.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 23, 2005 8:39 pm Fear of creepy fan-boys/girls?

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Wed Aug 24, 2005 1:25 am ZOMGZ my first post here. :shock: I change my mind and decided to continue loving Evangelion by coming here. :) Wow, some nice stuff there, nice pics(Loved the Gendo). I must take my friend there, sometime in the future. Looks like it would be a blast(for the ever exciteable moi)

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Aug 24, 2005 5:17 am That Gendo wins the 2005 Awesome Dude award for the radio thing. :idea:

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:07 am awwwwwwwwwwwww, why in america? i wanna go :cry: spending hundreds of Â£s on useless crap is what i was born to do! (no shit, i'm exercising that right tomorrow, heh heh) those cosplay pilots......i think i'll keep my comment to myself

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:33 am Well, its...cosplay...

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:42 am that would probably cover it. was that eva ipod purchased there or was he just showing it off?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Wed Aug 24, 2005 9:16 am Kaworu fangirl spotted, commencing termination program... Nah. Nice pictures, Digi, and welcome, Magami!

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:06 am @Space Penis: He certainly deserves that award. People kept turning around wondering where the music was coming from. @Ryo: Thanks. LOL. Is she the first Kaworu fangirl in these parts? @Magami: Welcome aboard! I dig the sig and avatar. @Ice Reaper: Soluzar might make the trek next year. Hop on the plane with him and join us.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:23 am @Ice Reaper: Soluzar might make the trek next year. Hop on the plane with him and join us.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:24 am I'm so jealous of you! You are so lucky living there.. There's no chance something similar would happen here in Greece.. :cry: Anyway Otakon must be a great experience! Nice pics Digitalex! Is you somewhere on the pics btw?
